constructors.name: Float
constructors.summary: Constructs a [float] as a copy of the given [float].

constructors.name: Float
constructors.summary: Converts a [String] to a [float], following the same rules as [method String.to_float].

constructors.name: Float
constructors.summary: Cast a [bool] value to a floating-point value, [code]float(true)[/code] will be equal to 1.0 and [code]float(false)[/code] will be equal to 0.0.

constructors.name: Float
constructors.summary: Cast an [int] value to a floating-point value, [code]float(1)[/code] will be equal to [code]1.0[/code].

operators.name: operator !=
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if two floats are different from each other. [b]Note:[/b] [constant @GDScript.NAN] doesn't behave the same as other numbers. Therefore, the results from this operator may not be accurate if NaNs are included.

operators.name: operator !=
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the integer has different value than the float.

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies each component of the [Color], including the alpha, by the given [float]. [codeblock] print(1.5 * Color(0.5, 0.5, 0.5)) # Prints (0.75, 0.75, 0.75, 1.5) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies each component of the [Quaternion] by the given [float]. This operation is not meaningful on its own, but it can be used as a part of a larger expression.

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies each component of the [Vector2] by the given [float]. [codeblock] print(2.5 * Vector2(1, 3)) # Prints (2.5, 7.5) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies each component of the [Vector2i] by the given [float]. Returns a [Vector2]. [codeblock] print(0.9 * Vector2i(10, 15)) # Prints (9.0, 13.5) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies each component of the [Vector3] by the given [float].

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies each component of the [Vector3i] by the given [float]. Returns a [Vector3]. [codeblock] print(0.9 * Vector3i(10, 15, 20)) # Prints (9.0, 13.5, 18.0) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies each component of the [Vector4] by the given [float].

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies each component of the [Vector4i] by the given [float]. Returns a [Vector4]. [codeblock] print(0.9 * Vector4i(10, 15, 20, -10)) # Prints (9.0, 13.5, 18.0, -9.0) [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies two [float]s.

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Multiplies a [float] and an [int]. The result is a [float].

operators.name: operator **
operators.summary: Raises a [float] to a power of a [float]. [codeblock] print(39.0625**0.25) # 2.5 [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator **
operators.summary: Raises a [float] to a power of an [int]. The result is a [float]. [codeblock] print(0.9**3) # 0.729 [/codeblock]

operators.name: operator +
operators.summary: Adds two floats.

operators.name: operator +
operators.summary: Adds a [float] and an [int]. The result is a [float].

operators.name: operator -
operators.summary: Subtracts a float from a float.

operators.name: operator -
operators.summary: Subtracts an [int] from a [float]. The result is a [float].

operators.name: operator /
operators.summary: Divides two floats.

operators.name: operator /
operators.summary: Divides a [float] by an [int]. The result is a [float].

operators.name: operator <
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the left float is less than the right one. [b]Note:[/b] [constant @GDScript.NAN] doesn't behave the same as other numbers. Therefore, the results from this operator may not be accurate if NaNs are included.

operators.name: operator <
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this [float] is less than the given [int].

operators.name: operator <=
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the left float is less than or equal to the right one. [b]Note:[/b] [constant @GDScript.NAN] doesn't behave the same as other numbers. Therefore, the results from this operator may not be accurate if NaNs are included.

operators.name: operator <=
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this [float] is less than or equal to the given [int].

operators.name: operator ==
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if both floats are exactly equal. [b]Note:[/b] Due to floating-point precision errors, consider using [method @GlobalScope.is_equal_approx] or [method @GlobalScope.is_zero_approx] instead, which are more reliable. [b]Note:[/b] [constant @GDScript.NAN] doesn't behave the same as other numbers. Therefore, the results from this operator may not be accurate if NaNs are included.

operators.name: operator ==
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the [float] and the given [int] are equal.

operators.name: operator >
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the left float is greater than the right one. [b]Note:[/b] [constant @GDScript.NAN] doesn't behave the same as other numbers. Therefore, the results from this operator may not be accurate if NaNs are included.

operators.name: operator >
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this [float] is greater than the given [int].

operators.name: operator >=
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the left float is greater than or equal to the right one. [b]Note:[/b] [constant @GDScript.NAN] doesn't behave the same as other numbers. Therefore, the results from this operator may not be accurate if NaNs are included.

operators.name: operator >=
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this [float] is greater than or equal to the given [int].

operators.name: operator unary+
operators.summary: Returns the same value as if the [code]+[/code] was not there. Unary [code]+[/code] does nothing, but sometimes it can make your code more readable.

operators.name: operator unary-
operators.summary: Returns the negative value of the [float]. If positive, turns the number negative. If negative, turns the number positive. With floats, the number zero can be either positive or negative.
